geisha.name: Geisha
description: ## Geisha
‹SOURCE Ultimate Magic/27›

In some cultures, the professional entertainer is a prestigious role. Specially trained entertainers called geisha are praised for their appearance and skill at conversation, music, dancing, singing, poetry, and calligraphy. A geisha provides social intimacy and status but not physical intimacy.

::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=down-magic note="Geisha are proficient in all simple weapons and one ‹class/monk› weapon. Geisha are not proficient in any armor or shield; unlike bards, geisha are subject to arcane spell failure even when casting in light armor or when using a shield." alter="Armor and weapon proficiencies"}

::ab[Tea Ceremony (Su)]{icon=boost ability="By spending 10 minutes preparing an elaborate tea ceremony, a geisha may affect her allies with *inspire courage, inspire competence, inspire greatness,* or *inspire heroics.* The ceremony's effects last 10 minutes. The geisha must spend 4 rounds of bardic performance for each creature to be affected."}

::ab[Geisha Knowledge]{icon=boost passive="A geisha adds ‹misc/half› her class level (minimum 1) on Craft (calligraphy) checks, Diplomacy checks, Knowledge (nobility) checks, and one type of Perform check (act, dance, oratory, percussion, string, or sing); she may make checks with these skills untrained." replace="Bardic knowledge"}
::ab[Scribe Scroll]{icon=power ability="A geisha gains ‹feat/Scribe Scroll› as a bonus feat."}

hatharat_agent.name: Hatharat Agent
description: ## Hatharat Agent
‹SOURCE Qadira, Jewel of the East/47›
Although the Hatharat employs all manner of specialists, bards who take up the role of agent are among its most infamous and widespread.

:::ab{l=1 icon=power action=Ability title="Called Favor (Ex)" replace="Bardic knowledge" imp7="An agent need only wait 1d6 days between uses of called favors. In addition to the favors above, he can now ask for a favor that supplies the aid of a single 5th-level specialist for 24 hours. This specialist is treated as a short-term ‹feat/cohort»<leadership› for the agent, but should remain under the control of the GM. The exact statistics for the specialist (who can be of any class) should also be calculated by the GM." imp9="The specialist can now be 6th-level." imp11="The specialist can now be 7th-level." imp13="The specialist can now be 8th-level." imp15="The specialist can now be 9th-level." imp17="The specialist can now be 10th-level." imp18="The specialist can now be 11th-level." special="You can find numerous pregenerated NPCs via the links at the bottom of the ‹main/Monsters« and NPCs page›."}

A Hatharat agent can use his contacts to ask for a favor from various individuals who have benefited from Hatharat aid in the past. The agent must spend 1d4 hours in an urban area with a population of at least 200 people to use this ability, and must succeed at a DC 20 Diplomacy check to secure the favor. Once an attempt to call in a favor has been made, whether or not the Diplomacy check was successful, the Hatharat agent must wait 2d6 days before attempting to call in a new favor, so as to not overtax the favors owed the society as a whole.

The Hatharat agent can use a successful called favor to secure an introduction to an important NPC, either to learn an important piece of information (something that would otherwise require a successful DC 20 Knowledge check to know) or to secure a pardon for a minor crime of which an ally has been convicted. Similar favors could be granted at the GM's discretion.

::ab[Informed Persuasion (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost flavor="A Hatharat agent uses his knowledge of a target to increase his persuasiveness." passive="If he has at least 1 rank in an associated Knowledge skill, he applies his Intelligence modifier (in addition to his Charisma modifier, as normal) to Bluff, Diplomacy, and Intimidate checks against that Knowledge skill's associated category of targets." special="The types of Knowledge and their associated groups are arcana (spellcasters), geography (tribal citizens), local (urban citizens), nobility (politicians and nobles), and religion (worshipers of a recognized faith)." replace=Well-versed}
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="A Hatharat agent gains the bardic performance listed below."}

::ab[Master of Manipulation (Su)]{next icon=power l=8 flavor="A Hatharat agent gains a reputation of being able to sense lies." ability="When using bardic performance, his very presence can often draw the truth out of others. This ability affects all creatures selected by the bard within a 20-foot-radius ‹misc/emanation› from the bard. Any affected creature can't speak any deliberate or intentional lies, but is aware of the effect and can choose to avoid answering a question to which it would normally respond with a lie, or may be evasive as long as it remains within the boundaries of the truth." special="This is a mind-affecting compulsion effect." replace="Dirge of doom"}

hoaxer.name: Hoaxer
description: ## Hoaxer
‹SOURCE Black Markets/29›
Hoaxers specialize in creating valuable-looking counterfeits and infusing these false treasures with dangerous magic to make their marks more vulnerable to future swindles.

::ab[Counterfeiter (Ex)]{icon=boost ability="A hoaxer adds ‹misc/half› his bard level (minimum 1) on all Appraise, Bluff, and Sleight of Hand checks, as well as on Craft, Knowledge, Linguistics, Perception, and Profession checks to create or detect a counterfeit or forgery. He can attempt such skill checks untrained." replace="Bardic knowledge"}
::ab[Bardic Performance (Su)]{head icon=power note="A hoaxer dedicates his arcane energies to deceiving others rather than to entertaining them, granting him the following unique performances."}

::ab[Bad Deal (Su)]{next icon=magic flavor="A hoaxer can use his performance to coax a bit of luck (good or bad) into a valuable-seeming object." provokes=Yes standard="The hoaxer can use 1 round of bardic performance to invest a single object in his possession with a hex he knows. This object must be light enough to hold in one hand, and cannot already have any magical properties. The object remains hexed as long as the hoaxer continues to spend 1 round of bardic performance each round as a @HLswift_action to maintain it, until the hoaxer drops the item, or until triggered. The hex triggers immediately upon being willingly accepted by another creature, targeting its new owner." imp1="The hoaxer knows one ‹ability/witch »hex<es› from the following list: ‹hex/blight›, ‹hex/charm›, ‹hex/evil eye›, ‹hex/fortune›, ‹hex/healing›, ‹hex/misfortune›, ‹hex/slumber›, or ‹hex/unnerve beasts›." repeatAt="He can learn one additional hex.~3~6~9~15~18" imp12="He can learn one additional hex, and the list of hexes grows to include ‹hex/agony›, ‹hex/hoarfrost›, ‹hex/ice tomb›, ‹hex/infected wounds›, ‹hex/nightmares›, ‹hex/retribution›, and ‹hex/speak in dreams›." special="A bad deal cannot be forced on a creature or slipped into its possession without its knowledge, but a hoaxer can lie or cheat to convince his mark to accept the object, or use spells like *beguiling gift.* The save DC for a hoaxer's hexes is equal to 10 + 1/2 the hoaxer's bard level + his Charisma modifier. These hexes function identically to witch hexes, except they can only target single creatures, and the hoaxer can only use his hexes by investing them into objects. If the hoaxer learns hexes from any other source (such as the ‹class/witch› class), he cannot invoke them into an object." replace="Inspire courage, inspire competence, and inspire greatness"}

::ab[Buyer Beware (Su)]{next icon=magic standard="A hoaxer can convince someone to accept a gift or trade as per ‹spell/beguiling gift› (DC = 10 + 1/2 the hoaxer's bard level + his Charisma modifier) by spending 1 round of bardic performance on a supernatural sales pitch. The hoaxer can use this ability to convince a creature to accept a hexed object created with his bad deal performance." replace=Countersong}

::ab[Personal Guarantee (Su)]{next icon=magic ability="A hoaxer can prevent his hexed wares from afflicting a target until he is safely out of sight. He can spend up to 1 round of bardic performance per bard level while hexing an object. For every round expended, the object's hex is delayed for 1 minute before triggering and hexing its current owner." replace=Distraction}

::ab[Curse Breaker (Su)]{next icon=magic l12="A hoaxer can use performance to create an effect equal to ‹spell/break enchantment›. This otherwise functions as ‹performance/soothing performance›." replace="Inspire greatness"}

::ab[Misery (Ex)]{l=2 flavor="A hoaxer learns to take joy in the misfortune of others." icon=boost ability="He gains a +1 morale bonus on attack rolls and damage rolls against creatures suffering from a curse, hex, or harmful mind-affecting effect, as well as a +1 morale bonus on Will saving throws against spells from such creatures." increment="p!These bonuses~-1~6" replace="Versatile performance"}
::ab[Versed in Curses (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def passive="A hoaxer gains a +4 bonus on saving throws against curses, hexes, and language-dependent effects." replace=Well-versed}

::ab[Curse Crafter (Ex)]{l=5 icon=power-magic ability="A hoaxer learns how to craft cursed items. He gains one ‹main/item creation feat› as a bonus feat." special="He can use these feats only to craft cursed items. In addition, he can craft items that appear magical but have no true magical properties - as if permanently affected by the ‹spell/magic aura› spell - for 50 gp." repeat="He gains another bonus item creation feat.~11~6" replace="Lore master"}

impervious_messenger.name: Impervious Messenger
description: ## Impervious Messenger
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/20›

The impervious messenger is capable of harnessing the unique qualities of bardic magic to keep, transport, and communicate the most precious of secrets and messages, even when subject to the most vigorous forms of observation, be they magical or mundane.
::ab[Bardic Performance (Su)]{head icon=info note="An impervious messenger gains the following types of bardic performances."}

::ab[Chant of Perfect Recall (Su)]{next icon=magic ability="An impervious messenger can spend 1 round of his bardic performance to commit something to memory. This ability functions exactly as per ‹spell/memorize page›, but the impervious messenger is the only recipient and he requires only 1 round to commit a single page to memory. An impervious messenger can commit additional pages worth of material to memory by using additional rounds of his bardic performance, but he can never memorize more than half his total bard level in pages (minimum 1)." replace=Fascinate}

::ab[Song of Subterfuge (Su)]{next l=6 icon=magic ability="An impervious messenger learns to use his performances to project various thoughts into his own mind to disrupt divination attempts. This acts similarly to ‹performance/distraction›, except it only affects the impervious messenger and allows him to use the result of his Perform check in place of saving throws against attempts to read his mind, such as ‹spell/detect thoughts›." imp18="If the impervious messenger succeeds on a saving throw while using *song of subterfuge,* the character who attempted the divination effect must succeed on a Will save (DC = 20 + the impervious messenger's Charisma modifier) or else the impervious messenger instantly learns the nature of the effect and can allow the effect to continue, but provide whatever information he chooses to the divining character rather than the true results of the divination effect." replace="Suggestion~mass suggestion"}

::ab[Unbroken Stride (Su)]{next l=8 icon=boost ability="An impervious messenger draws upon his bardic abilities to imbue himself with grace and speed. While using this bardic performance, an impervious messenger gains an insight bonus equal to ‹misc/half› his bard level on all Acrobatics, Climb, Fly, and Ride checks. In addition, while maintaining this performance, he gains the benefits of the ranger's ‹ability/woodland stride› ability and a +10-foot enhancement bonus to his base land speed." imp12="An impervious messenger using his *unbroken stride* bardic performance also acts as if under the effect of ‹spell/freedom of movement›. In addition, the enhancement bonus to his base land speed increases to +30 feet." replace="Dirge of doom~frightening tune"}

::ab[Cryptic Whisper (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost-def flavor="The impervious messenger internalizes his understanding of language and ciphers, and filters them through his bardic abilities." passive="He adds half his bard level to all Linguistics checks and Bluff checks to deliver secret messages, and he gains a +4 bonus on saving throws against ‹main/glyph spells, »symbol spells,› and language-dependent effects." replace="Bardic knowledge~well-versed"}

juggler.name: Juggler
description: ## Juggler
‹SOURCE Ranged Tactics Toolbox/7›

Jugglers are masters of manipulating objects, most famously by keeping multiple objects in the air simultaneously. They often incorporate this talent into acting, comedy, or dance. Adventuring jugglers find their abilities translate well into the use of thrown weapons.

::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiencies]{icon=info note="A juggler is proficient with all simple weapons, plus martial thrown weapons. A juggler is not proficient with shields." alter="Weapon and armor proficiencies"}

::ab[Fast Reactions (Ex)]{icon=power l1="A juggler gains ‹feat/Deflect Arrows› as a bonus feat." l5="He gains ‹feat/Snatch Arrows› as a bonus feat." special="The juggler doesn't need to meet the prerequisites for these feats." l11="The juggler can use the ability granted by these feats an additional time each round (with a -5 penalty on his second attack roll)." l17="The juggler gains another use of these feats (with a -10 penalty on his third attack roll)." replace="Bardic knowledge~lore master"}

::ab[Combat Juggling (Ex)]{icon=power l=2 ability="A juggler can hold and wield (in other words, &quot&juggle&quot&) up to three items or weapons in his hands. The juggler must be able to hold and wield an object in one hand in order to juggle it. This ability doesn't grant the juggler additional attacks, though it does allow him to use different weapons as part of a ‹rule/full attack›. As long as he is juggling fewer than three objects, the juggler is considered to have a free hand (for the purposes of drawing a weapon, using somatic components, using Deflect Arrows, and so on)." imp6="The juggler can juggle four items or weapons at once." imp10="The juggler can juggle five items or weapons at once." imp14="The juggler can juggle six items or weapons at once." imp18="The juggler can juggle seven items or weapons at once." special="If the juggler is affected by any action or condition that would require a ‹rule/concentration› check while he is juggling, he must attempt a Sleight of Hand check to continue juggling and avoid losing concentration. The check's DC is the same as a concentration check, treating the spell level as twice the number of objects being juggled. If the juggler fails his Sleight of Hand check, he drops all juggled objects but one, determined randomly." replace="Versatile performance"}

::ab[Evasion (Ex)]{icon=def l2="A juggler gains ‹ability/evasion›, as the ‹class/rogue› class feature of the same name." l12="He gains ‹talent/improved evasion›, as the advanced rogue talent." replace="Well-versed~soothing performance"}

lotus_geisha.name: Lotus Geisha
description: ## Lotus Geisha
‹SOURCE Dragon Empires Primer/22›
The lotus geisha of Minkai are renowned for their powers of seduction, and tales tell of lotus geisha disarming foes and bolstering allies. The lotus geisha is an archetype of the bard class.

::ab[Weapon Proficiency]{icon=info note="A lotus geisha is proficient with all simple weapons, plus the ‹eq-weapon/butterfly sword›, ‹eq-weapon/fighting fan›, ‹eq-weapon/iron brush›, ‹eq-weapon/sai›, ‹eq-weapon/shortbow›, and ‹eq-weapon/shuriken›." alter="Weapon proficiencies"}
:::ab{l=2 icon=power action=Ability title="Enrapturing Performance (Su)" imp7="This becomes a @HLmove_action." imp13="This becomes a @HLswift_action." replace=Well-versed}

A lotus geisha gains the ability to execute an *enrapturing performance* in addition to her bardic performance ability. An *enrapturing performance* is exactly like a bardic performance with the following exceptions.

A lotus geisha can use an *enrapturing performance* to recreate the effects of any of her known bardic performances, but she focuses the performance on only a single target within range. While other creatures see and hear a lotus geisha's *enrapturing performance,* only the target of this ability is affected by it. A lotus geisha cannot have a bardic performance and an *enrapturing performance* in effect at the same time. Every round spent engaged in an *enrapturing performance* counts against the total number of rounds per day she can use her bardic performance, and she cannot use this ability if she does not have any more rounds of bardic performance left for that day. Starting an *enrapturing performance* is a @HL[standard action].

A lotus geisha adds a +2 bonus to the DC of Will saves made to resist the effects of her *fascinate, frightening tune,* and *suggestion enrapturing performances.* When a lotus geisha uses her *enrapturing performance* ability to emulate *inspire competence, inspire courage, inspire greatness,* or *inspire heroics,* any bonuses to AC or on ability checks, attack rolls, damage rolls, saving throws, and skill checks increase by +1. For example, a lotus geisha using ‹performance/inspire heroics› as an *enrapturing performance* would grant her target a +5 morale bonus on saving throws and a +5 dodge bonus to AC, instead of the usual +4 bonuses.

::ab[Bonus Feats]{icon=power l1="A lotus geisha gains ‹feat/Spell Focus› (enchantment) as a bonus feat." l5="She gains ‹feat/Greater Spell Focus› (enchantment) as a bonus feat." replace="Bardic knowledge~lore master"}

luring_piper.name: Luring Piper
description: ## Luring Piper
‹SOURCE Monster Hunter's Handbook/11›

Some bards have an exceptional ability to use their musical performances to entrance the world's wilder creatures. These performers are so focused that they create melodies capable of leading animals and fey to their unwilling death.
::cskill{gain=sur}

::ab[Fey Secrets]{icon=boost passive="A luring piper adds ‹misc/half› his class level (minimum 1) on Bluff, Knowledge (nature), Perception, Sense Motive, and Survival checks when interacting with fey." replace="Bardic knowledge"}

::ab[Luring Presentation (Ex)]{icon=lower-down passive="Creatures that have the animal or fey type take a -2 penalty on saving throws imposed by a luring piper's bardic performances. Creatures with any other type gain a +2 bonus on saving throws against a luring piper's bardic performances." alter="Bardic performance"}

::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="The luring piper gains the following types of bardic performances that can only be made when using Perform (wind) and a when employing a wind instrument."}

::ab[Charming Melody (Su)]{next icon=power ability="While using the ‹performance/fascinate› bardic performance, the luring piper can play a song that compels animals and fey to follow him wherever he goes. Instead of sitting peacefully and merely observing the luring piper, creatures with the animal or fey type instead quietly and calmly walk toward the luring piper, stopping once they are adjacent to the luring piper and following him if he moves." alter=Fascinate}

::ab[Deadly Lure (Sp)]{next l=8 icon=boost ability="The luring piper can use his ‹performance/suggestion› bardic performance on targeted animals and fey to make them take actions that result in their own injury or death, such as leaping off a cliff or into a river. The targeted creature is allowed a second saving throw to shake off the effect before completing any self-harming actions." replace="Dirge of doom"}

::ab[Fey-Wounding Song (Sp)]{next icon=power l=12 fullround="The luring piper can expend 3 rounds of bardic performance to play a song that causes fey tremendous pain. Treat this as equivalent to ‹spell/mass inflict serious wounds›, but affecting only fey targets and using the lurid piper's bard level as his caster level." replace="Soothing performance"}

::ab[Piper's Attention (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def-roll passive="Whenever a luring piper is actively using Perform (wind) as part of a bardic performance, he gains a +4 bonus on saving throws against language-dependent, mind-affecting, and sonic effects. If this effect is caused by a fey creature, the luring piper can also roll the saving throw twice and take the better result." replace=Well-versed}

magician.name: Magician
description: ## Magician
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/82›
A magician dabbles in performance, but sees it as a means to tap into universal energies and channel them.
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="A magician gains the following types of bardic performance."}

::ab[Dweomercraft (Su)]{next icon=boost ability="A magician can use performance to manipulate magical energies. Allies of the magician gain a +1 bonus on caster level checks, ‹rule/concentration› checks, and attack rolls with spells and spell-like abilities." increment="This bonus~-1~6" special="This ability relies on visual and audible components." replace="Inspire courage"}

::ab[Spell Suppression (Su)]{next icon=magic l=8 flavor="A magician can use performance to counter the spells of his foes." ability="Once the bard begins using this performance, he tracks the number of rounds it has been in use. While performing, as an @HLimmediate_action, he can attempt to counter any spell that he can identify using Spellcraft, so long as that spell's level is equal or less than the total number of rounds he has been performing spell suppression. The attempt to counter the spell is made as if using ‹spell/dispel magic›, using the bard's level as the caster level. If successful, the bardic performance immediately ends." special="This ability requires audible components." replace="Dirge of doom"}

::ab[Metamagic Mastery (Ex)]{next icon=magic-boost l=14 ability="A magician can use performance to apply a ‹main/metamagic feat› to a spell he is about to cast without increasing the casting time. The bard must still expend a higher-level slot to cast this spell. This causes the performance to immediately end." special="This ability requires audible components." replace="Frightening tune"}
::ab[Magical Talent (Ex)]{icon=boost passive="A magician gains a bonus equal to ‹misc/half› his level on Knowledge (arcana), Spellcraft, and Use Magic Device checks." replace="Bardic knowledge"}
::ab[Improved Counterspell]{icon=power ability="A magician gains ‹feat/Improved Counterspell› as a bonus feat." replace=Countersong}

::ab[Extended Performance (Su)]{l=2 icon=boost swift="A magician can extend the duration of bardic performance after he stops concentrating by sacrificing a spell slot. The performance effect lingers for 1 extra round per level of the spell." special="Only one spell may be sacrificed per performance, and performance types that take affect after a specific number of rounds cannot be extended." replace=Well-versed}

::ab[Expanded Repertoire (Ex)]{icon=learn l2="A magician can add one spell to his spells known from the spell list of any arcane spellcasting class. The spell must be of a level he can cast." l6="He can add another spell, following the same limitations." l10="He can add another spell, following the same limitations." l14="He can add another spell, following the same limitations." l18="He can add another spell, following the same limitations." replace="Versatile performance"}
::ab[Arcane Bond (Ex)]{l=5 head icon=boost ability="A magician gains the *arcane bond* ability as a ‹class/wizard›. He may not choose a familiar or a weapon as a bonded item." replace="Lore master"}
:::ab{next title="Arcane Bond: Bonded Object" flavor="Relevant text from the wizard ability." icon=magic action=Ability}

If you select a bonded object, you begin play with one at no cost. Objects that are the subject of an arcane bond must fall into one of the following categories: amulet, ring, staff, wand, or weapon. These objects are always masterwork quality. If the object is an amulet or ring, it must be worn to have effect, while staves, wands, and weapons must be wielded. If you attempt to cast a spell without your bonded object worn or in hand, you must make a ‹rule/concentration› check or lose the spell. The DC for this check is equal to 20 + the spell's level. If the object is a ring or amulet, it occupies the ‹rule/ring or »neck slot› accordingly.

A bonded object can be used once per day to cast any one spell that you have in your spellbook and are capable of casting, even if the spell is not prepared. This spell is treated like any other spell cast by you, including casting time, duration, and other effects dependent on your level. This spell cannot be modified by metamagic feats or other abilities. The bonded object cannot be used to cast spells from your opposition schools.

You can add additional magic abilities to your bonded object as if you have the required item creation feats and if you meet the level prerequisites of the feat. For example, a wizard with a bonded dagger must be at least 5th level to add magic abilities to the dagger (see ‹feat/Craft Magic Arms and Armor›). If the bonded object is a wand, it loses its wand abilities when its last charge is consumed, but it is not destroyed and it retains all of its bonded object properties and can be used to craft a new wand. The magic properties of a bonded object, including any magic abilities added to the object, only function for the wizard who owns it. If a bonded object's owner dies, or the item is replaced, the object reverts to being an ordinary masterwork item of the appropriate type.

If a bonded object is damaged, it is restored to full hit points the next time you prepare your spells. If the object of an arcane bond is lost or destroyed, it can be replaced after 1 week in a special ritual that costs 200 gp per wizard level plus the cost of the masterwork item. This ritual takes 8 hours to complete. Items replaced in this way do not possess any of the additional enchantments of the previous bonded item. You can designate an existing magic item as your bonded item. This functions in the same way as replacing a lost or destroyed item except that the new magic item retains its abilities while gaining the benefits and drawbacks of becoming a bonded item.

::ab[Wand Mastery (Ex)]{icon=boost l10="When a magician uses a wand containing a spell on his spell list, he uses his Charisma bonus to set the wand's save." imp16="When using such a wand, he uses his caster level in place of the wand's caster level." replace="Jack of all trades"}

masked_performer.name: Masked Performer
description: ## Masked Performer
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/20›

Not merely an entertainer in a disguise, the masked performer takes on the persona represented by the likeness she wears. Her alter ego might be a theatrical archetype, a legendary hero, or a revered spirit. Masked performers are often the stars of religious plays and festivals, and aficionados easily recognize them and associate them with their most famous role.

::ab[Dual Identity (Ex)]{icon=power ability="This ability acts as the ‹class/vigilante› ability of the same name, except as follows. The masked performer's identities are referred to as social and masked, rather than social and vigilante. Unlike a vigilante, most people who know of the masked performer actually know that the masked performer's identities are one and the same. Nonetheless, the dual identity ability is valuable to the masked performer due to offering her two perspectives on any problem and two different alignments." replace="Bardic knowledge"}
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="A masked performer gains the following types of bardic performance."}

::ab[Seamless Guise (Ex)]{next icon=power-boost ability="A masked performer learns to behave in a way that appears perfectly proper and normal for her current role, on stage or off. She can maintain this performance for 1 hour for every 3 rounds of bardic performance she spends. While this performance is active, she receives a +10 bonus on Disguise and Perform (act) checks to remain in character after she has already established her character. For instance, this bonus does not apply against the initial Perception check when meeting a creature paying attention to her but does apply on the automatic additional Perception check each hour of interacting." replace=Countersong}

::ab[Exaggerated Pose (Su)]{next icon=boost l=3 order=aw ability="A masked performer can enhance her own physical and social abilities by simply altering her posture. When she starts this performance, the masked performer chooses a Strength-, Dexterity-, or Charisma-based skill and gains a +2 competence bonus on checks with that skill as long as she maintains this performance." imp7="The bonus becomes +3." imp11="The bonus becomes +4." imp15="This bonus becomes +5." imp19="The bonus becomes +6." swift="The masked performer can change the chosen skill." special="*Exaggerated pose* is an emotion effect and relies on visual components." replace="Inspire competence"}

::ab[Stage Combat (Su)]{next icon=power l=6 ability="A masked performer gains the benefit of a ‹main/combat feat› she doesn't have as long as she maintains this performance. The masked performer chooses the feat when she starts this performance. The masked performer must meet all the feat's prerequisites." special="As usual when temporarily gaining a feat, if the feat she chooses has any limitations on uses, such as ‹feat/Stunning Fist«'s› daily uses, all uses of that combat feat while maintaining *stage combat* count toward that feat's limit. In the case of Stunning Fist, if the masked performer gained that feat again later the same day, the expended uses would remain expended. *Stage combat* relies on visual components." replace=Suggestion}

::ab[Multiplicity of Masks (Su)]{next icon=magic-aura l=18 ability="A masked performer can cause any number of her allies to appear as herself or as another ally within 60 feet. To be affected, an ally must be within 60 feet of the masker performer and able to see her. As long as the masked performer maintains this performance, enemies perceive all affected allies as the chosen character. If an affected ally is a different size or creature type than the chosen character, enemies can attempt a Will save (DC = 10 + ‹misc/half› the masked performer's bard level + her Charisma modifier) to disbelieve. Otherwise, there is no saving throw, but a successful attack against a disguised ally automatically reveals the ally's true form." special="If an ally moves out of range, that ally reverts to his original form, and the entire effect ends if the ally providing his appearance to the others moves out of range. *Multiplicity of masks* is an illusion (glamer) effect that relies on visual components." replace="Mass suggestion"}

::ab[Social Grace (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power ability="A masked performer gains the ‹soctalent/social grace› vigilante social talent." special="She gains an additional skill with *social grace* 1 level later than a vigilante (at 6th level, 10th level, 14th level, and 18th level)." replace="Versatile performance"}

::ab[Masked Talents]{icon=power l5="A masked performer gains a rogue talent from the following list: ‹talent/expert leaper›, ‹talent/fast stealth›, ‹talent/ledge walker›, ‹talent/rogue crawl›, ‹talent/rope master›, ‹talent/stand up›, and ‹talent/wall scramble›." l17="A masked performer can choose any of the above talents or an advanced rogue talent from the following list: ‹talent/defensive roll›, ‹talent/fast tumble›, ‹talent/hide in plain sight›, and ‹talent/skill mastery›." special="This ability does not qualify her for the ‹feat/Extra Rogue Talent› feat." replace="Lore master"}

mute_musician.name: Mute Musician
description: ## Mute Musician
‹SOURCE Horror Realms/9›

A mute musician forswears speech for the unnatural songs and thunderous silences of the depths of space. She learns to blend light and sound in seemingly impossible ways. Some mute musicians look to the darker aspects of ‹faith/Desna› as their inspiration for these performances, but most mute musicians find their muse in the alien sounds of the Elder Mythos and the entities that orbit the blind idiot god ‹faith/Azathoth›, who floats in the heart of the Dark Tapestry.

::ab[Mute (Ex)]{icon=down ability="A mute musician has a disability or injury (possibly self-inflicted or even psychological) that prevents her from speaking or vocalizing. Language-dependent effects (including Perform &#91&oratory&#93&) require the bard to be able to use telepathy, nonverbal languages, or writing to communicate. She cannot use Perform (sing) or speak languages, though she can still create audible bardic performances by means of a musical instrument. A mute musician can provide verbal and somatic components for spells she casts via any musical instrument in which she has ranks in the appropriate Perform skill."}
::ab[Eschew Materials (Ex)]{icon=power ability="A mute musician gains ‹feat/Eschew Materials› as a bonus feat at 1st level." replace="Bardic knowledge"}
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="A mute musician gains the following bardic performances."}

::ab[Symphony of Silence (Su)]{next icon=aura-protect l=3 ability="The mute musician's music muffles all other sounds within a 30-foot radius. All creatures in the area of effect gain a +2 bonus on all saving throws made against sonic attacks or language-dependent effects." imp7="The bonus becomes +3." imp11="The bonus becomes +4." imp15="The bonus becomes +5." imp19="The bonus becomes +6." special="Symphony of silence relies on audible components." replace="Inspire competence"}

::ab[Maddening Harmonics (Su)]{next icon=aura-lower l=14 ability="The mute musician can create a performance so baffling and discordant that it usurps all thought within a 30-foot radius with chaos and entropy. The mute musician can select which creatures in this area are affected by the maddening harmonics and which are not. All targeted creatures within this area must succeed at a Will saving throw (DC = 10 + ‹misc/half› the bard's level + the bard's Charisma modifier) or be confused for as long as they can hear the performance." special="If the creature succeeds at its saving throw, it is immune to this ability for 24 hours. This performance relies on audible components." replace="Frightening tune"}

::ab[Ceaseless Performance (Su)]{next icon=power l=15 ability="The mute musician can continue taking the @HLfree_action to maintain a bardic performance even while confused, cowering in fear, dazed, nauseated, panicked, paralyzed, petrified, silenced, staggered, stunned, or unconscious. Even if the mute musician is killed, she can continue to take the free action to maintain her performance as long as she has rounds remaining. Only the utter destruction of the mute musician's body (such as via ‹spell/destruction› or ‹spell/disintegrate›, or by reducing the bard to a negative hit point total equal to 10 &times& her Constitution score) causes the performance to end." replace="Inspire heroics"}

::ab[Song of the Conjunction (Su)]{next icon=magic l=18 ability="The mute musician can harmonize with the alien chorus beyond the music of the spheres, creating a portentous cosmic alignment. This effect duplicates a ‹spell/gate› used to travel, save that the destination point must be on the same plane as the bard, but it is not otherwise limited by distance." replace="Mass suggestion"}

::ab[Insights from Beyond (Ex)]{l=2 icon=learn choice="A mute musician adds two abjuration, conjuration (calling), conjuration (summoning), or conjuration (teleportation) spells from the ‹main/spells_>sorcerer›/‹main/spells_>wizard› spell list to her list of bard spells known." imp6="The bard can choose two more spells to add to her spells known." repeat="The bard can choose two more spells to know.~10~4" replace="Versatile performance"}

::ab[Dulled Horror (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def passive="At 2nd level, a mute musician gains a +4 bonus on saves against confusion, fear, insanity effects, and the supernatural abilities of aberrations." replace=Well-versed}

::ab[Eldritch Caesura (Su)]{l=10 icon=boost ability="A mute musician can insert unsettling silences into her otherworldly music by spending an additional 1 round of bardic performance per round. This supernatural technique impossibly blurs the line between music and light, transmitting audible performances and sonic bard spells through most barriers save lead. This allows the music and sonic spells to affect creatures across planar boundaries (including on the ‹rule/Ethereal<_plane› or ‹rule/Shadow Plane«s›), vacuums, and areas of magical silence." replace=Jack-of-all-trades}

::ab[Ex-Mute Musicians]{icon=warning note="A mute musician who regains the ability to speak or chooses to speak aloud loses all abilities granted by this archetype. She can regain the archetype's abilities by spending 24 hours without speaking."}

negotiator.name: Negotiator
description: ## Negotiator
‹SOURCE Champions of Balance/18›
Bards are experts at manipulating the emotions and perceptions of others. Negotiators are those bards who take the skills of a performer and apply them to the realms of business and law.

::ab[Hard Bargainer]{icon=boost passive="A negotiator adds ‹misc/half› his class level (minimum 1) on Bluff, Diplomacy, Intimidate, Knowledge (local), and Sense Motive checks." replace="Bardic knowledge"}
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="A negotiator gains the following types of bardic performance."}

::ab[Counterargument (Su)]{next icon=magic ability="This performance acts as ‹performance/countersong›, except a negotiator can use only Perform (act, comedy, oratory, or sing) to perform a counterargument."}

::ab[Fast Talk (Su)]{next icon=lower l=1 ability="A negotiator can use performance to affect the perceptions of any number of targets that can hear him. While the negotiator performs, these targets take a -1 penalty on saving throws against enchantment (charm) and illusion (figment, glamer, or shadow) effects, as well as a penalty on Appraise checks equal to half the negotiator's level (minimum -1). A target that fails its Appraise check believes that the object in question is worth 10% more or less (negotiator's choice) than its actual value." imp5="The penalty on saving throws becomes -2 and the percentage becomes 20%." imp11="These become -3 and 30%." imp17="These become -4 and 40%." special="Fast talk is a language-dependent, mind-affecting ability that uses audible components." replace="Inspire courage"}

::ab[Binding Contract (Sp)]{next l=9 icon=magic ability="The negotiator can bind another creature's promise to the negotiator with a geas. This acts as ‹spell/lesser geas›, except the creature must have Hit Dice equal to or less than the negotiator's level. The target can negate this effect with a successful Will save (DC = 10 + 1/2 the negotiator's level + the negotiator's Charisma modifier). The geas is discharged if the negotiator breaks his end of the bargain. Using this ability requires 3 rounds of continuous performance, and the target must be able to see and hear the bard throughout the performance." imp18="This effect acts as ‹spell/geas/quest› (no HD limit, and the target cannot attempt a saving throw)." special="Binding contract is an enchantment (compulsion), mind-affecting, language-dependent ability that relies on audible components." replace="Inspire greatness"}

::ab[Rogue Talents]{icon=power l2="A negotiator gains a ‹ability/rogue talent›, as the rogue class feature of the same name." l6="The negotiator gains another talent." l10="The negotiator gains another talent." l14="The negotiator gains another talent." l18="The negotiator gains another talent." special="A negotiator cannot select a rogue talent that modifies the sneak attack ability." replace="Versatile performance"}

::ab[Master of Rhetoric (Ex)]{l=5 icon=power ability="The negotiator can take 10 on any Bluff, Diplomacy, Intimidate, Knowledge (local), or Sense Motive check if he has ranks in that skill. A negotiator can choose not to take 10 and can instead roll normally. In addition, once per day, the negotiator can take 20 on one of those skill checks (without spending any additional time)." imp11="He can take 20 twice a day." imp17="He can take 20 three times a day." replace="Lore master"}

::ab[Advanced Talents]{icon=boost l10="A negotiator can now select an ‹ability/advanced rogue talent› in place of a rogue talent. This also applies to the talents gained at 14th and 18th levels." special="A negotiator cannot select an advanced talent that modifies the sneak attack ability."}

phrenologist.name: Phrenologist
description: ## Phrenologist
‹SOURCE Occult Adventures/114›
The phrenologist is an expert at reading creatures' skulls, and he learns to use this knowledge to his benefit.

::ab[Phrenological Knowledge (Ex)]{icon=power-boost ability="A phrenologist is an expert at reading creatures' skulls. He gains ‹feat/Psychic Sensitivity› as a bonus feat, and he can use the phrenology skill unlock even if he is untrained in ‹skill/Knowledge (arcana)›." order=ap passive="He adds ‹misc/half› his class level (minimum 1) on all Knowledge (arcana) checks for the phrenology occult skill unlock." replace="Bardic knowledge"}

::ab[Skull-Versed (Ex)]{icon=lower l=2 ability="A phrenologist can use knowledge gleaned from a creature's skull to tailor a performance. All creatures on which the phrenologist has successfully used his phrenology skill unlock take a -2 penalty on all saving throws against the phrenologist's bardic performances." replace=Well-versed}

::ab[Phrenological Savant]{icon=boost l=10 ability="A phrenologist gains either ‹feat/Psychic Maestro› or ‹feat/Psychic Virtuoso› as a bonus feat. If he chooses Psychic Maestro, he must select phrenology as one of his two skill unlocks to use an additional time." replace=Jack-of-all-trades}
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="A phrenologist gains the following types of bardic performance."}

::ab[Skull Sonata (Su)]{next icon=aura ability="A phrenologist learns to use the resonance of other creatures' skulls to increase sonic damage. All enemies within 30 feet of the phrenologist that have skulls take an amount of additional sonic damage equal to half the phrenologist's bard level (minimum 1) each time they take sonic damage." special="*Skull sonata* relies on audible components." replace="Inspire courage"}

::ab[In Your Head (Sp)]{next icon=magic l3="A phrenologist can use his performance to send his own senses into another's skull, as the spell ‹spell/witness› (DC = 10 + 1/2 the phrenologist's class level + his Charisma modifier)." l11="This performance's duration increases to 10 minutes per bard level." l19="The duration increases to 1 hour per bard level." special="*In your head* relies on audible components." replace="Inspire competence"}

::ab[Fingers of Fascination (Su)]{next icon=power ability="When a phrenologist uses the ‹performance/fascinate› bardic performance, he can send forth psychic fingers along with his performance, allowing him to study one of the fascinated creatures with his phrenology skill unlock without touching its head physically and without it being helpless, willing, or paralyzed. This does not spend his daily use of the phrenology skill unlock." alter=Fascinate}

pitax_academy_of_grand_arts.name: Pitax: Academy of Grand Arts
description: ## Pitax: Academy of Grand Arts
‹SOURCE Guide to the River Kingdoms/40›

Bards who have successfully mastered the strict curriculum of Pitax's Academy of Grand Arts to graduate are more adept in their assigned field of performance, at the expense of versatility in other areas. Such bards have the *focused performance* alternate class ability, which replaces *versatile performance.*

::ab[Focused Performance (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power order=ca choice="A bard must choose one type of Perform skill." ability="The bard gains ‹feat/Extra Performance› as a bonus feat, usable only with the chosen category of the Perform skill." imp8="The bard gains Extra Performance as an additional bonus feat." imp14="The bard gains Extra Performance again as an additional bonus feat." imp20="The bard again gains Extra Performance as an additional bonus feat." special="The additional uses of bardic performance gained from the bonus feats cannot be used with any category of the Performance skill other than the one chosen at 2nd level." replace="Versatile performance"}

plant_speaker.name: Plant Speaker
description: ## Plant Speaker (Vine Leshy only)
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/22›
Plant speakers build upon their racial *plantspeech* ability and their storytelling tendencies to create an art form full of deep metaphors and allegory rather than specific details and facts.
::prereq{r="Vine leshy"}
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="Plant speakers gain the following bardic performance."}

::ab[Leshy Speaker (Su)]{next icon=magic l9="The plant speaker can speak to its disembodied brethren, the spirits of nature that become leshys. He performs for 10 minutes and spends 7 rounds of bardic performance to gain the effects of ‹spell/commune with nature›." replace="Inspire greatness"}

::ab[Plant Speech]{icon=boost-power l=1 passive="A plant speaker's mind-affecting bard spells and class abilities also affect plants, ignoring plants' immunity to mind-affecting effects." imp2="A plant speaker's racial plantspeech allows him to communicate with all plants, not just plants that match his alternate form." replace="Bardic knowledge~well-versed"}

::ab[Mystical Allegory (Su)]{icon=power-magic l5="The plant speaker can tell a story that matches some of the themes of the current situation in order to gain insight, gaining the effects of ‹spell/augury› by performing for 1 minute and spending 4 rounds of bardic performance." l11="He can perform for 10 minutes and spend 7 rounds of bardic performance to gain the effects of ‹spell/divination›." l17="He can perform for 1 hour and spend 10 rounds of bardic performance to gain the effects of ‹spell/legend lore›, but the information is always vague and incomplete." replace="Lore master"}

prankster.name: Prankster
description: ## Prankster
‹SOURCE Advanced Race Guide/35›

The prankster sees humor as the highest form or art, and pranks as the highest form of humor. In addition to setting friends up for light-hearted pranks, the prankster can use his quick wit and cruel sense of humor to enrage foes before incapacitating them with a clever turn of phrase.
::prereq{r=Gnome}
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=info note="A prankster's bardic performance functions like a bard's, but some of its performances are exchanged for those listed below."}

::ab[Mock (Su)]{next icon=lower l1="The prankster can use his performance to cause one creature to become furious with him. The creature to be mocked must be within 90 feet, able to see, hear, and understand the prankster, and capable of paying attention to him. The prankster must also be able to see the creature affected. A creature within range receives a Will save (DC 10 + ‹misc/half› the prankster's level + the prankster's Charisma modifier) to negate the effect. If a creature's saving throw succeeds, the prankster cannot successfully mock that creature for 24 hours. If its saving throw fails, the creature is angered by the performance and seeks to harm the prankster. While the prankster maintains the mocking, the target takes a -2 penalty on all attack rolls and skill checks until it has successfully attacked the prankster with a melee or ranged attack, or has harmed the creature with a spell that deals damage." l4="He can mock two people at once." l7="He can mock three creatures at once." l10="He can mock four at once." l13="He can mock five creatures at once." l16="He can mock six at once." l19="He can mock seven people at once." special="Mock is an enchantment (compulsion) mind-affecting ability. Mock relies on audible and visual components in order to function." replace=Fascinate}

::ab[Punchline (Sp)]{next l=6 icon=power-magic ability="The prankster can use his performance to tell a punchline to amuse a creature he has already *mocked* (see above), goading it into ‹spell/hideous laughter› (as the spell). Using this ability does not disrupt the *mock* effect, but it does require a @HLstandard_action to activate (in addition to the @HLfree_action to continue the *mock* effect). This ability affects only a single creature. A prankster can use this ability more than once against an individual creature during an individual performance." special="Telling a punchline does not count against a prankster's daily use of bardic performance. A Will saving throw (DC 10 + 1/2 the bard's level + the bard's Charisma modifier) negates the effect. Punchline is an enchantment (compulsion), mind affecting, language-dependent ability and relies on audible components." replace=Suggestion}

::ab[Mass Punchline (Sp)]{next l=18 icon=boost ability="This ability functions just like *punchline,* but allows a prankster of 18th level or higher to use *hideous laughter* simultaneously against any number of creatures that he has mocked." replace="Mass suggestion"}

::ab[Swap (Ex)]{icon=power ability="A prankster can steal an object from a creature and replace it with another object of the same size or smaller that the prankster has in his hand. This functions as the ‹rule/steal› combat maneuver, but the prankster does not provoke an attack of opportunity, and may use his Sleight of Hand check in place of his combat maneuver check. If the prankster's check exceeds the target's CMD by 10 or more, the target is unaware the swap has been made until it tries to use the swapped object or the end of its next turn (whichever happens first)." replace="Lore master"}

provocateur.name: Provocateur
description: ## Provocateur
‹SOURCE Inner Sea Intrigue/45›

Provocateurs are bards who use their art to make controversial political statements, undermine enemies' reputations, and upset the status quo on a large scale. They can be found most often in areas of political unrest such as the unruly Chelish city of Kintargo, throughout rebellion-fraught Galt, and among the backstabbing upper classes of Absalom, Brevoy, and Taldor.

::ab[Provocateur (Ex)]{icon=boost passive="A provocateur adds ‹misc/half› his bard level on skill checks to reduce a target's ‹rule/influence› and on skill checks to reduce a target's attitude toward another creature or organization." replace="Bardic knowledge"}

::ab[Calumny (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power ability="A provocateur can use his Perform (comedy, oratory, or sing) modifier in place of his Bluff and Diplomacy modifier on checks to ‹rule/spread a rumor› and in place of his Intimidate modifier on checks to demoralize foes." replace="The versatile performance gained at 2nd level"}

::ab[Damning Performance (Su)]{icon=power l4="A provocateur can cause all observers fascinated by her ‹performance/fascinate› performance to become less friendly to a target creature or group of her choice for 10 minutes per bard level. Affected observers' attitudes toward the target, the target's influence with affected observers, and affected observers' trust level as ‹rule/contacts› for the target are all reduced by one step. If she reveals information during the performance that would reduce those creatures' attitudes anyway, those attitudes worsen by an additional step for the duration. If the provocateur engages in a ‹rule/verbal duel<s› against the target with an audience affected by damning performance, she automatically gains two edges." l18="The duration increases to 1 day per bard level." replace="Suggestion~mass suggestion"}

ringleader_ag.name: Ringleader (AG)
description: ## Ringleader (AG)
‹SOURCE Adventurer's Guide/33›

The ‹rule/Aspis Consortium«'s› global operations are too extensive to conduct without relying on contractors and hirelings. Ringleaders are specialists who oversee large operations in which trouble could strike at any moment and any place.

::ab[Cunning Plan (Su)]{icon=boost order=an ability="By spending 1 minute laying out a plan, a ringleader can impart inspiring instructions to a number of allies equal to her bard level + her Charisma bonus. Upon concluding the plan, the ringleader chooses one of her bardic performances and expends a number of rounds of bardic performance equal to up to double her bard level. Thereafter, as a @HLmove_action, the ringleader or any of the instructed allies can trigger the implanted instructions, causing that creature to immediately gain the benefits of the ringleader's implanted bardic performance (each creature can trigger the instructions for itself separately). This effect lasts for a number of rounds equal to the rounds of bardic performance the ringleader expended while creating the plan, though the effect also ends if the ringleader dismisses it as a @HLfree_action or activates a different plan or bardic performance." special="For the purpose of this ability, the ringleader attempts any skill checks associated with the bardic performance once a creature triggers the plan. A ringleader's cunning plans all expire each time she regains her daily spell slots or after 24 hours (whichever comes first)." note="A ringleader is less capable of improvising, and when she uses bardic performance without creating a cunning plan, she must expend 1 additional round of bardic performance to begin the performance." imp7="The ringleader (but not an ally) can trigger a cunning plan as a @HLswift_action instead of as a move action." imp13="The ringleader can trigger a cunning plan as an @HLimmediate_action." alter="Bardic performance"}

::ab[Never Lose Face (Ex)]{icon=def l=2 passive="A ringleader is never outdone by her minions. She gains a +2 bonus on saving throws against effects that also target one or more of her allies." replace=Well-versed}

::ab[Sinister Mien (Ex)]{icon=power-boost imp2="A ringleader can use her Intimidate bonus in place of her Perform bonus when using her bardic performance class ability. When the ringleader uses Intimidate to improve a creature's attitude, the creature's attitude remains friendly for 10 additional minutes per point by which the ringleader exceeded the check's DC." imp6="The ringleader gains ‹feat/Dazzling Display› as a bonus feat and does not need a weapon in her hand to use the feat." imp10="The ringleader can activate Dazzling Display as a @HLstandard_action." imp14="She can affect all enemies within 60 feet with Dazzling Display." imp18="The range increases to 120 feet." replace="Versatile performance"}

::ab[Countless Contingencies (Su)]{icon=power imp6="Whenever a ringleader creates a plan using her cunning plan ability, she can choose two different bardic performances. When she triggers the plan, she chooses which of the two performances takes effect." imp12="A ringleader can spend a @HLstandard_action and 2 rounds of bardic performance to extend the duration of a triggered plan by 1d4 rounds." imp18="The ringleader can choose three different bardic performances when using her cunning plan ability." replace="Suggestion~mass suggestion~soothing performance"}

ringleader_ui.name: Ringleader (UI)
description: ## Ringleader (UI)
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/21›

Ringleaders are adept in getting the most out of their allies, and they excel at planning for unexpected complications in advance. Often the masterminds behind elaborate heists and infiltrations, ringleaders use their organizational abilities to make sure the plan goes without a hitch, even when the team is split up and unable to communicate with each other on the spot.

::ab[Inspire Competence (Su)]{icon=boost l3="A ringleader can use his performance to help an ally succeed at a task. This ability acts as the ‹performance/inspire competence› bardic performance with the following additions." l8="The ringleader's ally gains a competence bonus on an additional skill (for a total of two skills). In addition, he can end the effect to reroll a check with one of the affected skills (the reroll still applies the bonus from inspire competence). Each ally can only use this latter ability once per day, regardless of the number of skills affected and the number of times they are affected by inspire competence." l14="The ringleader's ally gains a competence bonus on an additional skill (for a total of three skills). In addition, he can take 10 on the affected skills when stressed or distracted." alter="Inspire competence" replace="Dirge of doom~frightening tune"}
:::ab{title="Inspired Plan (Su)" icon=boost l=2 action=Ability replace="Versatile performance"}

A ringleader can alter his performance so it affects a single ally at a later time. The ringleader must explain his plan to that ally for a number of minutes equal to the number of bardic performance rounds he expends on the effect, and use audible and/or visual components according to the performance altered. This costs the ringleader the chosen number of rounds of bardic performance.

At any time before the ringleader recovers bardic performance rounds for the day, the inspired character can activate the performance as a @HL[standard action] wherever she is. When activated, the altered performance affects only the inspired ally and has no audible or visible components. If the performance could normally affect any number of allies, its effects last 2 rounds for each round the ringleader spent on the performance. Otherwise, the effects last 1 round for each round the ringleader spent on the performance.

A ringleader can alter only the *countersong, distraction, inspire courage, inspire competence, inspire greatness,* and *inspire heroics* performances in this way. The ringleader makes any necessary choices when establishing the performance, though in the case of *countersong* and *distraction,* the ringleader attempts Perform checks once his ally triggers the plan, ignoring any temporary bonuses or penalties, no matter whether they applied when he explained the plan or when the ally triggers it.

A character can be subject to only one inspired plan at a time and cannot be subject to a ringleader's inspired plan and any of the ringleader's normal performances at the same time, but she can benefit from other bardic performances in the interim. The ringleader cannot use inspired plan on himself, but he can have dormant plans ready to trigger on as many different allies as he can afford to affect at once.

::ab[Hidden Plans (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def passive="A ringleader becomes a master of discretion. The ringleader gains a +4 bonus on saving throws against effects that would read his thoughts or compel him to tell the truth." replace=Well-versed}

::ab[Prepared (Ex)]{l=5 icon=power ability="A ringleader expects the unexpected. When the ringleader is in a situation where he could use a particular piece of nonmagical gear, the ringleader can attempt a Knowledge check appropriate to his current situation to have purchased that item in advance. The DC of the check is equal to the item's cost in gp. If he succeeds, he deducts the appropriate cost of the item from his current gp total and now has the item in question on his person. He cannot use this ability to retroactively gain an item that would change his encumbrance to a different load category, though in this case, he can attempt to retrieve such objects from a pack mule, extradimensional storage space, or other location that would not affect his encumbrance." useInc=bard~5 useF="5~1" replace="Lore master"}

sandman.name: Sandman
description: ## Sandman
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/82›
Combining performance with stealth, trickery, and guile, the sandman uses cleverness to keep others off-balance.
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="A sandman gains the following types of bardic performance."}

::ab[Stealspell (Su)]{next icon=magic ability="A sandman can use performance to steal spells from his foes and add them to his list of spells known. Once the performance is started, the bard can steal a prepared spell or a spell known from another creature with a touch attack as a standard action. The target receives a Will save (DC 10 + ‹misc/half› the bard's level + the bard's Cha bonus) to negate the effect. The sandman may choose a spell to steal, but if the target does not possess the spell, the bardic performance immediately ends. Otherwise the spell stolen is random, but it is always of the highest level that the bard can cast, if possible. The target loses the prepared spell or spell known and the sandman adds it to his list of spells known for as long as the performance continues, after which it reverts to the original recipient." order=an note="While stolen, the bard can cast the spell using his available spell slots. This use does not consume the stolen spell. If the bard steals another spell while a spell is stolen, the previous spell immediately reverts to its original owner." special="This ability requires visual components." replace="Inspire courage"}

::ab[Slumber Song (Sp)]{next icon=magic l6="A sandman can use his performance to cause a creature he has already *fascinated* to fall asleep (as ‹spell/deep slumber›, but with no HD limit). Otherwise, this ability functions like ‹performance/suggestion›." replace=Suggestion}

::ab[Dramatic Subtext (Su)]{next icon=magic l9="A sandman can use bardic performance to cast spells without obvious visual or audible components while retaining the spell's normal effects. Observers must succeed at a Perception check opposed by a sandman's Sleight of Hand check to notice that the sandman is the source of the spellcasting (though spellcasting still provokes attacks of opportunity)." special="The bard must use this performance for at least 2 rounds before casting a spell; otherwise he is automatically detected and the performance ends." replace="Inspire greatness"}

::ab[Greater Stealspell (Su)]{next icon=boost l15="A sandman's *stealspell* power improves. When a target fails a save against his *stealspell* performance, the sandman discovers its spell resistance (if any) and all spells it has prepared or knows. He can then choose which spell to steal. The sandman may forgo stealing a spell and instead reduce the target's SR by an amount equal to half his bard level and gain that amount of spell resistance for as long as he continues performing." special="If he steals additional spell resistance, it stacks with previously stolen SR. If he steals a spell or ceases performing, the spell resistance immediately reverts back to its owner." replace="Inspire heroics"}

::ab[Mass Slumber Song (Sp)]{next icon=boost l18="A sandman can use *slumber song* to affect any number of *fascinated* creatures within 30 feet. Otherwise, this ability functions like ‹performance/mass suggestion›." replace="Mass suggestion"}

::ab[Spell Catching (Su)]{next icon=def-magic l=20 immediate="A sandman who saves against a spell or spell-like ability that targets only him (not including area spells) may respond with his bardic performance. He must attempt a caster level check (DC 10 + the spell's original caster level). If it succeeds, the sandman can absorb the spell effect without harm and immediately recast that spell (using the original caster's level and save DC) or any spell he knows of that level or lower." special="Using this ability consumes a number of rounds of bardic performance equal to the spell's level, even if the check fails." replace="Deadly performance"}

::ab[Master of Deception (Ex)]{icon=boost-power passive="A sandman gains a bonus equal to half his level on Bluff, Sleight of Hand, and Stealth checks." order=pa ability="He may also disarm magical traps with Disable Device as a rogue's ‹ability/trapfinding› ability." replace="Bardic knowledge"}

::ab[Sneakspell (Ex)]{icon=boost l2="A sandman adds +1 to the save DC of spells and bardic performance against opponents who are denied their Dex bonus." l6="He gains a +2 bonus on caster level checks to overcome spell resistance against such foes." l10="The save DC now increases by +2." l14="The bonus on caster level checks becomes +4." l18="The save DC now increases by +3." replace="Versatile performance"}

::ab[Trap Sense (Ex)]{icon=def l=3 passive="A sandman gains a bonus on Reflex saves against traps and a dodge bonus to AC against traps. These bonuses are equal to one-third his bard level." replace="Inspire competence"}

::ab[Sneak Attack (Ex)]{icon=boost ability="A sandman inflicts extra damage against targets within 30 feet that he flanks or that are denied their Dex bonus to AC against him. This damage is equal to +1d6 points for every five bard levels he has (e.g, a 15th-level sandman would deal +3d6 extra damage)." replace="Lore master"}

savage_skald.name: Savage Skald
description: ## Savage Skald
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/84›
Far from civilization, furious tribes have their own war-singers, work-chanters, and lore-keepers, savaging enemies with song and sword alike.
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="A savage skald gains the following types of bardic performance."}

::ab[Inspiring Blow (Su)]{next icon=boost flavor="A savage skald roars his war-cries with each telling blow." immediate="When he confirms a critical hit, he can start this performance (ending any other performances). He gains ‹rule/temporary hit points› equal to his Charisma modifier (if positive), and all allies within 30 feet gain a +1 morale bonus on their next attack roll prior to the start of his next turn. These temporary hit points remain until the bard ends his performance." replace=Fascinate}

::ab[Incite Rage (Su)]{next icon=magic l=6 ability="A savage skald can induce a furious rage in one creature within 30 feet. This effect functions as a ‹spell/rage› spell that lasts as long as the target can hear the bard's performance; however, unwilling creatures can be affected if they fail a Will save (DC 10 + ‹misc/half› the bard's level + the bard's Cha modifier). Success renders the target immune to this power for 24 hours. The bard cannot target himself with this ability." special="If the target has the rage class feature, it can instead immediately rage and stay in this rage without consuming rounds of rage per day as long as the bard continues performing. This mind-affecting effect requires audible components." replace=Suggestion}

::ab[Song of the Fallen (Sp)]{next icon=magic l10="A savage skald can duplicate the effect of a ‹magic-wondrous/horn of Valhalla›. This effect requires 10 continuous rounds of performance. This summons barbarians as a silver horn." l13="This summons barbarians as a brass horn." l16="This now summons as a bronze horn." l19="This now summons as an iron horn." special="The warriors remain only as long as the bard continues his performance. This ability requires audible components." replace="Jack of all trades"}

::ab[Berserkergang (Ex)]{next icon=protect l12="A savage skald can inspire a rapturous battle trance that suppresses pain, stunning, and fear effects for one creature." l15="This affects two creatures at once." l18="This now affects three creatures at once." special="Affected creatures also gain DR 5/- (DR 10/- against nonlethal damage); this benefit stacks with the damage reduction class ability of barbarians. This mind-affecting ability requires audible components." replace="Soothing performance"}
::ab[Battle Song (Su)]{next icon=magic l18="A savage skald can affect all allies within 30 feet when using performance to *incite rage.*" replace="Mass suggestion"}

sea_singer.name: Sea Singer
description: ## Sea Singer
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/84›
The sea singer calls the blue waters his home, and is much in demand among sea captains wishing good fortune for their crew and hull as they ply the tradewinds far and wide.
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="A sea singer gains the following types of bardic performance."}

::ab[Sea Shanty (Su)]{next icon=boost ability="A sea singer learns to counter seasickness and exhaustion during long sea voyages. Each round of a sea shanty, he makes a Perform skill check. Allies within 30 feet (including the sea singer) may use his Perform check in place of a saving throw against becoming exhausted, fatigued, nauseated, or sickened; if already under such an effect, a new save is allowed each round of the sea shanty, using the bard's Perform check for the save." special="A sea shanty has no effect on instantaneous effects or effects that do not allow saves. This ability requires audible components." replace=Countersong}

::ab[Still Water (Su)]{next icon=magic l3="A sea singer can use performance to calm rough waters within 30 feet, reducing the DC for Profession (sailor) and Swim checks, as well as for Acrobatics and Climb checks aboard ship, by an amount equal to the bard's level (to a minimum of DC 10) for as long he continues to perform. He can extend this duration to 1 hour by playing for 10 consecutive rounds." special="This ability requires audible components." replace="Inspire competence"}

::ab[Whistle the Wind (Su)]{next icon=magic l6="A sea singer can use performance to create a ‹spell/gust of wind›. This wind lasts for as long as he continues his performance. He can extend this duration to 1 minute by playing for 5 consecutive rounds." replace=Suggestion}

::ab[Call the Storm (Su)]{next icon=magic l18="A sea singer can use performance to duplicate ‹spell/control water›, ‹spell/control weather›, ‹spell/control winds›, or ‹spell/storm of vengeance›, using his bard level as the caster level. Using this ability requires 1 round of continuous performance per level of the spell (as if he were a ‹class/druid›). These effects continue for as long as the bard continues performing (the effects of *control weather* happen immediately), but not longer than the spell's normal duration." replace="Mass suggestion"}

::ab[World Traveler (Ex)]{icon=boost-roll passive="A sea singer gains a bonus equal to ‹misc/half› his bard level on Knowledge (geography), Knowledge (local), Knowledge (nature), and Linguistics checks." ability="He can reroll a check against one of these skills, but must take the result of the second roll even if it is worse." order=pau useInc=bard~5~+ useF="1~1~5~~5" replace="Bardic knowledge"}

::ab[Familiar]{l=2 icon=power ability="A sea singer acquires an exotic pet - a ‹monster/monkey› or parrot (treat as ‹monster/raven›) - that gains abilities as a wizard's ‹ability/familiar›, using the sea singer's bard level as his wizard level." replace="Versatile performance"}

::ab[Sea Legs (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def passive="A sea singer gains a +4 bonus on saving throws against air and water effects and effects that would cause the sea singer to slip, trip, or otherwise be knocked ‹rule/prone›. He gains a +2 bonus to CMD against grapple, overrun, and trip." replace=Well-versed}

shadow_puppeteer.name: Shadow Puppeteer
description: ## Shadow Puppeteer
‹SOURCE Advanced Race Guide/210›
A shadow puppeteer invokes amazing and terrifying shadow puppet shows, producing supernatural effects by creating and manipulating shadow. A shadow puppeteer has the following class feature.
::prereq{r=Wayang}

::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="A shadow puppeteer gains the following types of bardic performance. The character must be able to perform shadow puppetry in order to activate any of these abilities. Shadow puppetry uses Perform (act), and requires a light source." replace="Inspire courage~inspire competence"}

::ab[Shadow Servant (Sp)]{next icon=magic ability="The puppeteer can create a shadow servant to perform simple tasks. The shadow servant is identical to an ‹spell/unseen servant› (caster level equal to the shadow puppeteer's bard level), except it appears as a formless shadow." special="Shadow servant relies on visual components."}

::ab[Shadow Puppets (Sp)]{next icon=magic ability="A shadow puppeteer can use bardic performance to create one quasi-real shadowy creature resembling a monster from the ‹spell/summon monster I› list. These shadowy creatures otherwise work like ‹spell/shadow conjuration›, and targets interacting with them get a Will saving throw (DC 10 + ‹misc/half› their bard level + their Charisma bonus) to treat them as only 20% real." incrementRoman="This ability acts like ‹spell/summon~1~3" incrementDesc=monster incrementEnd="›."}

silver_balladeer.name: Silver Balladeer
description: ## Silver Balladeer
‹SOURCE Occult Adventures/115›

The bright purity of silver makes beautiful music, and its vibrations are also anathema to unnatural creatures. Some bards use a mixture of silver-stringed instruments and esoteric knowledge to battle the dark forces of the world.
::prereq{other="A silver balladeer must have a good alignment."}

::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="A silver balladeer gains the bardic performances listed below. These bardic performances all require the use of a silver or silver-stringed instrument, which costs twice as much as a normal ‹eq-misc/masterwork<_musical> instrument›."}

::ab[Break Curse (Su)]{next icon=protect l6="A silver balladeer can suppress a single curse affecting an ally within 30 feet that is able to see and hear her. Each round the silver balladeer uses *break curse,* she attempts a Perform check against the original DC of the curse. If successful, she suppresses the curse for 1 round. After she has suppressed the curse for 4 consecutive rounds, she can attempt a caster level check to remove the curse as if she had cast ‹spell/remove curse›." replace=Suggestion}

::ab[Holy Vibration (Su)]{next icon=magic l9="A silver balladeer can spend a round of bardic performance to make a single door or a window within 30 feet resonate with a holy vibration. Undead and creatures with the evil subtype are unable to open a door or window affected in this way (as ‹spell/arcane lock›) for 10 minutes per bard level. If such a creature has the incorporeal subtype, it is unable to move through the affected door or window, or through any walls, floors, or ceilings within 20 feet of it." special="Incorporeal creatures can attempt Charisma checks to break through areas warded by holy vibration, using the same break DC as for the object the creature is trying to move through." replace="Inspire greatness"}

::ab[Mass Break Curse (Su)]{next icon=protect l18="A silver balladeer can suppress all curses among any number of allies within 30 feet when using *break curse.* Every 4 consecutive rounds of using this performance, the silver balladeer can attempt a caster level check to remove a single curse on a single ally affected by this performance, as if she had cast *remove curse.*" replace="Mass suggestion"}
::ab[Pure Heart (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def passive="A silver balladeer gains a +4 bonus on saving throws against curses, hexes, and charm effects." replace=Well-versed}

::ab[Silver Mastery (Su)]{l=2 icon=boost passive="A silver balladeer treats silver weapons as if they were also ‹eq-material/cold iron› for the purpose of overcoming damage reduction. Also, ‹eq-material/alchemical silver› weapons do not impose a penalty on damage rolls when wielded by a silver balladeer, and a silver balladeer gains a +1 bonus on attack rolls made with ‹eq-material/mithral› weapons." replace="The 2nd-level versatile performance"}

solacer.name: Solacer
description: ## Solacer
‹SOURCE Healer's Handbook/18›
Solacers are experts in the healing arts as well as creators of performances that console the distraught, rally the stricken, raise spirits, and vanquish sorrow.
::cskill{gain=heal lose=app}

::ab[Learned Physician (Ex)]{icon=boost-power l1="A solacer adds ‹misc/half› his class level (minimum 1) on Heal checks and can attempt Knowledge checks untrained." l5="A solacer can take 10 on checks with any Knowledge skill he has ranks in. The solacer can choose not to take 10 and can instead roll normally. In addition, once per day, the solacer can take 20 on any Heal check, though this does not affect the time it takes to perform the task attempted." l11="He can Take 20 twice a day." l17="He can Take 20 three times a day." replace="Bardic knowledge~lore master"}

::ab[Inspire Tenacity (Su)]{l=1 icon=protect ability="A solacer can use his bardic performances to bolster his allies' mental endurance when they need it most. All allies within 30 feet automatically stabilize if they are ‹misc/dying› and gain a +2 morale bonus on saving throws against mind-affecting effects, poisons, and disease." special="This performance relies on audible components." replace=Countersong}

::ab[Creative Treatment (Su)]{l=2 useInc=bard~4~2 useF="2~1~4" icon=roll-power order=ua ability="When a solacer attempts a Heal check and fails, he can immediately reroll the check and take the better result." special="Additionally, when acting as a confidante (or any other type of advisor) to ‹rule/reduc«e»<ing> sanity damage« with rest›, the solacer can attempt a Charisma check instead of an Intelligence or Wisdom check and add his Charisma modifier to the amount of sanity damage removed instead of his Intelligence or Wisdom modifier. When acting as a confidante (or any other type of advisor) to reduce a ‹rule/madness«'s› DC with rest, the solacer can attempt a Charisma check instead of an Intelligence or Wisdom check and reduce the madness's DC by 1/2 his Charisma modifier instead of 1/2 his Intelligence or Wisdom modifier." replace="Versatile performance"}

::ab[Invigorating Artistry (Su)]{l=10 icon=protect flavor="A solacer can use his performances to protect and heal a target's soul." ability="The solacer can perform for 1 hour (this requires no skill check) and grant several benefits for 24 hours to the creatures that can hear or see his performance, or both, depending on whether the performance has audible or visual components. Affected creatures gain a +3 bonus on saving throws against curses, possession spells and effects (such as ‹spell/magic jar›), and domination and mind control spells and effects (such as ‹spell/dominate person›). If the target is suffering any or multiple such effects, it immediately can attempt a new saving throw against each; the effect ends if the saving throw is successful. Only one such extra saving throw can be granted against each condition in any 24-hour period. If a creature benefits from the daily performance between saving throws against a ‹rule/corruption›, it gains a +3 bonus on the next saving throw against that corruption." imp16="This bonus increases to +4." imp19="This bonus increases to +5." replace=Jack-of-all-trades}

songhealer.name: Songhealer
description: ## Songhealer
‹SOURCE Ultimate Magic/27›
Words can harm, but they also heal. The songhealer brings peace and surcease of pain, calming wild emotions and providing a balm for the wounded body.

::ab[Enhance Healing (Su)]{icon=boost useMod=Cha ability="A songhealer can cause any healing effect from a spell completion or spell trigger item to function at a caster level equal to his class level." replace="Versatile performance"}
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="A songhealer gains the following type of bardic performance."}
