::ab[Healing Performance (Su)]{next icon=magic-aid l=14 ability="A songhealer can use his performance to create an effect equivalent to ‹spell/heal› on a living target (or ‹spell/harm› on an undead target), using the bard's level as the caster level. Using this ability requires 5 rounds of continuous performance, and the target must be able to see and hear the bard throughout the performance." special="The *healing performance* relies on audible and visual components." replace="Frightening tune"}

::ab[Funereal Ballad (Su)]{next l=20 icon=magic-aid ability="A songhealer can use his performance to create an effect equivalent to ‹spell/resurrection› on a dead creature, using the bard's level as the caster level. Using this ability requires 20 rounds of continuous performance, and the target must be within 10 feet of the bard for the entire performance." special="*Funereal ballad* relies on audible and visual components." replace="Deadly performance"}

sorrowsoul.name: Sorrowsoul
description: ## Sorrowsoul
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/22›

It isn't uncommon for a bard to, on occasion, sing mournful tunes or craft elegies that depict the deepest sadness or the most profound suffering. But while most bards who do so create such performances for fame or wealth, the sorrowsoul has known tragedy and loss on a level so intimate, he has bound it to his soul.

::ab[Lyric Sorrow (Su)]{head icon=power note="A sorrowsoul draws upon grief, rage, and tragedy to fuel his bardic performances. When a sorrowsoul begins a bardic performance, he can choose to alter its effects with *lyric sorrow.* If he does so, he uses twice the number of rounds of his bardic performance ability as normal, and even if the sorrowsoul has the ‹feat/Lingering Performance› feat, the performance's effects do not last for any additional rounds." alter="Bardic performance" replace="Soothing performance~versatile performance"}

::ab[Inspire Courage]{next icon=def-boost l1="A sorrowsoul can use his lyric sorrow in conjunction with ‹performance/inspire courage›. When he chooses to do so, he increases his bonuses on saving throws and on weapon attack and damage rolls from +1 to +2, but his other allies receive no benefit." l5="His bonuses become +4." l11="His bonuses become +6." l17="His bonuses become +8."}

::ab[Inspire Greatness]{next icon=boost-def l12="A sorrowsoul can use his lyric sorrow ability in conjunction with ‹performance/inspire greatness› to grant himself 3 bonus Hit Dice, a +3 competence bonus on attack rolls, and a +2 competence bonus on Fortitude saves; if he does so, *inspire greatness* only affects him, not any other allies."}

::ab[Inspire Heroics]{next icon=def l15="When a sorrowsoul uses his lyric sorrow ability in conjunction with the ‹performance/inspire heroics› bardic performance, he gains ‹umr/fast healing› 5 and benefits from a 50% miss chance, as if under the effects of ‹spell/displacement›, in addition to the standard bonuses conferred by this bardic performance. But if he does so, *inspire heroics* only affects him, not any other allies."}
::ab[Darkness Denied (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def passive="A sorrowsoul gains a +4 bonus on saving throws against negative energy and death effects." replace=Well-versed}

::ab[Spurn Harm (Su)]{icon=def flavor="A sorrowsoul can funnel his bardic powers to persevere in the face of seemingly certain death." immediate="A sorrowsoul can spend 3 rounds of his daily bardic performance to gain a +2 insight bonus on all saving throws for 1 round." imp11="A sorrowsoul also gains spell resistance equal to 11 + his bard level when using this ability." imp17="He also gains DR 10/- when using this ability." replace="Lore master"}

sound_striker.name: Sound Striker
description: ## Sound Striker
**Source** ‹source/Ultimate Magic« pg. 27›, [errata](https://paizo.com/paizo/faq/v5748nruor1fz#v5748eaic9t4f)
They say that words can cut deeper than any blade, and the sound striker proves this true. Using music and words as a weapon, he can focus his performances into a deadly delivery.
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="A sound striker gains the following types of bardic performance. Neither performance can be performed more quickly than a standard action."}

::ab[Wordstrike (Su)]{next icon=zap l=3 standard="The sound striker bard can spend 1 round of bardic performance to direct a burst of sonically charged words at a creature or object. This performance deals 1d4 points of damage plus the bard's level to an object, or half this damage to a living creature." replace="Inspire competence"}

::ab[Weird Words (Su)]{next icon=zap l6="The bard can start a performance that is always a @HLstandard_action to speak up to one word per 4 bard levels laden with sonic energy. Each word deals 4d6 points of sonic damage as a ranged touch attack with a range of 30 feet. The bard adds his charisma modifier on damage rolls with weird words. Multiple words that strike the same target stack into a single powerful attack, applying energy resistance and bonuses on damage rolls only once. The bard can target all words at the same or different targets, but he unleashes all words simultaneously. Each word costs 1 round of bardic performance." replace=Suggestion}

speaker_of_the_palatine_eye.name: Speaker of the Palatine Eye
description: ## Speaker of the Palatine Eye
‹SOURCE Heroes of Golarion/8›

Working from the shadows of Ustalav's high society, the Esoteric Order of the Palatine Eye[^NoSource] is a mystical cult built around the quest for immortality. Founded by a count who claimed to have been enlightened by angels, the Order follows a strict hierarchy, and membership is open only to the elite. The more money a member provides the Order, the more secrets he learns to aid his path toward angelic ascension.
A speaker of the Palatine Eye influences others using his occult knowledge rather than the boisterous enthusiasm of most bards.
::prereq{other="He must be a worshipper of ‹faith/Pharasma›, but he may worship additional deities as well."}

::ab[Angelic Grace (Ex)]{icon=skill-power ability="Members of the Order of the Palatine Eye carry themselves with the austerity and import their heavenly aspirations demand. A speaker of the Palatine Eye does not have Acrobatics or Escape Artist as class skills, but he gains Celestial as a bonus language. This does not count toward the number of languages he is otherwise able to learn." alter="The bard's class skills"}

::ab[Spells]{icon=magic note="A speaker of the Palatine Eye draws his powers from the mysterious rites of the Esoteric Order of the Palatine Eye. He casts psychic spells from the ‹main/spells_>mesmerist« list› instead of arcane spells from the bard spell list. He otherwise casts spells, learns spells, and determines his spell allotment and spell DCs as a bard." alter=Spells}

::ab[Corpse Speaker (Su)]{icon=magic ability="A speaker of the Palatine Eye can make it appear as if a corpse is speaking, similar to ‹spell/ventriloquism›. A creature must succeed at a Will saving throw (DC = 10 + ‹misc/half› the bard's level + the bard's Charisma modifier) to see through the ruse. A creature that fails the saving throw is convinced that the corpse is speaking of its own volition." replace=Countersong}

::ab[Keen Ritualist (Ex)]{icon=magic-boost l10="A speaker of the Palatine Eye reduces the number of secondary casters required to cast a ‹rule/occult_>ritual<s› by 2." l16="The DCs of skill checks the speaker of the Palatine Eye attempts during a ritual are reduced by 2." l19="A speaker of the Palatine Eye can perform any ritual by himself without needing to satisfy any secondary caster requirements. The DCs of skill checks he attempts during a ritual are reduced by an additional 2, for a maximum DC reduction of 4." replace=Jack-of-all-trades}
[^NoSource]: More information on the Esoteric Order of the Palatine Eye can be found in [Pathfinder Adventure Path #44: Trial of the Beast](source/pathfinder_44_trial_of_the_beast).

stonesinger.name: Stonesinger
description: ## Stonesinger
‹SOURCE Heroes of the Darklands/11›

Once practiced by the dwarves in their Quest for Sky, the traditions of the stonesinger bards changed when the dwarves arrived on the surface. The tradition still hearkens back to its subterranean origins, however, and is practiced as a form of meditation or worship by many dwarves. Stonesingers are rare bards capable of vocalizing their bardic performances through subsonic harmonies, allowing their performances to carry as subtle vibrations through stone, rather than through the air. They command considerable power underground.

::ab[Earth Magic]{icon=power-learn passive="A stonesinger is treated as having the ‹feat/Eschew Materials› feat so long as he is touching natural or manufactured stone. Additionally, a stonesinger adds the following spells to the bard spell list at the listed spell level." s1="Magic stone" s2="Stone shield" s3="Stone shape" s4="Earth glide" s5=Stoneskin s6="Stone tell" alter=Spellcasting}

::ab[Stone Song (Su)]{icon=power ability="A stonesinger's bardic performance is a subsonic vibration that resonates from his body and travels through solid rock. This vibration is subtle enough to not be heard, though creatures with the ‹umr/tremorsense› universal monster ability can distinctly hear the stone song out to a range of 100 feet per bard level. All allies within 30 feet of the stonesinger benefit from stone song, perceiving the effect due to proximity. An allied creature with tremorsense benefits from stone song as long as it's within the extended range, provided that there is an unobstructed path of solid earth between the creature and stonesinger. Natural and manufactured stone does not inhibit stone song's range." special="A stonesinger must use Perform (sing) or Perform (oratory) for this bardic performance." alter="Bardic performance"}

::ab[Tremor (Su)]{icon=power-lower ability="As part of another bardic performance, a stonesinger can cause the ground within 30 feet to tremble, throwing enemies off balance and causing them to take a -1 circumstance penalty to AC as long as the stonesinger continues performing." imp5="The penalty becomes -2." imp11="The penalty becomes -3." imp17="The penalty becomes -4." special="Creatures levitating or flying above the ground are unaffected by this ability." replace=Countersong}

::ab[Quake (Su)]{icon=aura l8="A stonesinger causes the ground to shudder and buckle with his song. All enemies within 30 feet of the stonesinger must succeed at a Reflex save (DC = 10 + ‹misc/half› the stonesinger's level + his Charisma bonus) when he begins a bardic performance or be knocked ‹rule/prone›." special="Prone and flying creatures, as well as creatures with the ‹umr/earth glide› universal monster ability, are unaffected by this performance." replace="Dirge of doom"}

street_performer.name: Street Performer
description: ## Street Performer
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/85›
Whether acrobat, troubadour, or thespian, the street performer mixes with the masses, singing for his supper.
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="A street performer gains the following types of bardic performance."}

::ab[Disappearing Act (Su)]{next icon=power-aura l1="A street performer can use performance to divert attention from an ally. All creatures within 30 feet that fail a Will save (DC 10 + ‹misc/half› the bard's level + the bard's Cha modifier) treat one creature chosen by the bard as if it were ‹rule/invisible›." l5="This performance now affects two allies." l11="The bard can render three allies invisible." l17="The bard can now affect four allies." special="If the targets take any action that would cause them to become visible, they become visible to everyone. The bard cannot use this ability on himself. This ability is a mind-affecting effect that requires visual components." replace="Inspire courage"}

::ab[Harmless Performer (Su)]{next icon=def l3="A street performer can use performance to appear meek and unworthy of being attacked. While using this performance, whenever an enemy targets the street performer, the enemy must succeed at a Will save (DC 10 + 1/2 the bard's level + the bard's Cha bonus) or be unable to attack the bard this round, as per ‹spell/sanctuary›. The enemy loses the attack that targeted the bard, but may spend additional attacks targeting other creatures. If the opponent was targeting the street performer with a spell, it must succeed at a ‹rule/concentration› check at the same DC or lose the spell. If this check succeeds, it may target another creature with the spell instead." special="This mind-affecting ability requires audible or visual components." replace="Inspire competence"}

::ab[Madcap Prank (Su)]{next icon=lower l9="A street performer can use performance to discomfit a target within 30 feet, causing its clothing to become tangled, its headgear to fall down over its eyes, or even causing it to slip and fall or otherwise be made to appear a fool. The target must make a Reflex save (DC 10 + 1/2 the bard's level + the bard's Cha modifier) each round that it hears or sees the performance, or it takes one of the following random effects each round: 1-‹misc/blinded›, 2-‹misc/dazzled›, 3-‹misc/deafened›, 4-‹rule/entangled›, 5-fall ‹rule/prone›, 6-‹misc/nauseated›. Each effect lasts 1 round." replace="Inspire greatness"}

::ab[Slip through the Crowd (Su)]{next icon=boost l15="A street performer's disappearing act enables affected creatures to move through crowd squares and enemy-occupied squares without impediment. Affected creatures are treated as if having ‹spell/greater invisibility›, but enemies gain a new saving throw to notice them each time they are attacked." replace="Inspire heroics"}

::ab[Gladhanding]{icon=boost ability="A street performer earns double the normal amount of money from ‹skill/Perform› checks." standard="He may use a Bluff check in place of a Diplomacy check to improve a creature's attitude for 1 minute, after which its attitude becomes one step worse than originally." order=as replace=Countersong}

::ab[Streetwise]{icon=boost passive="A street performer gains a bonus equal to half his level on Bluff, Disguise, Knowledge (local), and Sleight of Hand checks, Diplomacy or Intimidate checks made to influence crowds, and Diplomacy checks to gather information (minimum +1)." replace="Bardic knowledge"}

::ab[Quick Change (Ex)]{icon=power l=5 standard="A street performer can don a disguise by taking a -5 penalty on his check." swift="He can take 10 on Bluff and Disguise checks and use Bluff to create a diversion to hide." ability="He can Take 20 on a Bluff or Disguise check once per day." order=swa imp11="He can Take 20 twice a day." imp17="He can Take 20 three times a day." replace="Lore master"}

studious_librarian.name: Studious Librarian
description: ## Studious Librarian
‹SOURCE Inner Sea Intrigue/46›

Studious librarians are bards that have studied long in great libraries of the Inner Sea region, such as Taldor's ‹rule/Kitharodian Academy› in Oppara, Forae Logos and the ‹rule/Pathfinder Society«'s› Skyreach in Absalom, and the Grand Library of Eto in Osirion.
::ab[Scribe Scroll]{icon=boost ability="A studious librarian gains ‹feat/Scribe Scroll› as a bonus feat." replace=Distraction}

::ab[Comparative Arcane Studies (Su)]{icon=magic l=6 flavor="The studious librarian is exposed to a wide variety of magical writing, and can use his own magic to duplicate spells from other classes' spell lists." ability="A studious librarian can cast any spell from the ‹main/spells_>bard›, ‹main/spells_>sorcerer›/‹main/spells_>wizard›, or ‹main/spells_>witch› spell lists from a scroll or spellbook in hand as if it were one of his bard spells known, expending a bard spell slot of the same spell level to cast the desired spell. Casting a spell from a scroll this way does not cause the spell to vanish from the scroll. A spell cast in this way cannot be modified by metamagic." order=au useInc=bard~4~6 useF="6~1~4" replace=Suggestion~jack-of-all-trades}

::ab[Critical Research Focus (Ex)]{icon=boost l8="The studious librarian threatens a critical hit on ‹rule/a »Research« check› with a roll of 18, 19, or 20. He gains a +4 bonus on Research checks to confirm a critical hit." replace="Dirge of doom"}
::ab[Perfect Recollection (Ex)]{icon=boost l19="The studious librarian can always take 20 on Knowledge checks." replace="Mass suggestion"}

::ab[One with the Library (Ex)]{icon=learn l20="The studious librarian can replace one of her spells known with any bard spell from a scroll once per day by studying the scroll for 1 hour. Doing so erases the spell from the scroll." replace="Deadly performance"}

thundercaller.name: Thundercaller
description: ## Thundercaller
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/41;Varisia, Birthplace of Legends/11›

Thundercallers bear the knowledge of the ancients, wielding it like a weapon to protect the land for which they care so deeply. Their voices ring like thunder, calling the sky's wrath down upon their enemies and summoning furious rage to bolster their allies.

::ab[Bound to the Land (Ex)]{icon=boost passive="A thundercaller gains a bonus equal to ‹misc/half› her level on Handle Animal checks, Knowledge (nature) checks, and Survival checks." replace="Bardic knowledge"}
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="A thundercaller gains the following types of bardic performance."}

::ab[Thunder Call (Su)]{next icon=magic l3="The thundercaller can start a performance to unleash a deafening peal of thunder. This allows the thundercaller to spend a round of performance to create an effect similar to the spell ‹spell/sound burst› (having the same range and area and allowing the same saving throw)." l7="The sonic damage dealt by this blast of sound increases to 3d8." l11="The damage becomes 5d8." l15="The damage becomes 7d8." l19="The damage becomes 9d8." special="A creature that succeeds at its saving throw against thunder call is immune to the stunning effect but still takes sonic damage. Beginning this performance is always a @HLstandard_action." replace="Inspire competence"}

::ab[Incite Rage (Su)]{next icon=magic-aura l6="The thundercaller can induce a furious rage in one creature within 30 feet. This effect functions as the ‹spell/rage› spell and lasts as long as the target can hear the thundercaller's performance. Any unwilling creature targeted is affected if it fails a Will saving throw (DC = 10 + half the thundercaller's level + the thundercaller's Charisma modifier). A target that succeeds at its saving throw is immune to this power for 24 hours. The thundercaller cannot target herself with this ability." special="If the target has the rage class feature, it can instead immediately rage and stay in this rage without consuming rounds of rage per day as long as the thundercaller continues performing. This performance is a mind-affecting effect and relies on audible components." replace="Suggestion~mass suggestion"}

::ab[Call Lightning (Su)]{next icon=zap l8="The thundercaller can use her performance to summon bolts of lightning, as per the spell ‹spell/call lightning›. The lightning storm lasts for as long as she continues her performance, calling down one bolt of lightning per round as a @HLstandard_action. The damage of these lightning bolts can be increased in stormy areas or can be limited as *call lightning* describes." replace="Dirge of doom"}

::ab[Call Lightning Storm (Su)]{next icon=zap l14="The thundercaller can use her performance to summon bolts of lightning, as per the spell ‹spell/call lightning storm›. The lightning storm lasts for as long as she continues her performance, calling down one bolt of lightning per round as a @HLstandard_action. The damage of these lightning bolts can be increased in stormy areas or can be limited as *call lightning* describes." replace="Frightening tune"}

voice_of_brigh.name: Voice of Brigh
description: ## Voice of Brigh
‹SOURCE Construct Handbook/14›

The [Whisper in Bronze](faith/brigh) sometimes sends inspiration to her favorite followers in the form of music. These songs hold the notes of creation and unmaking that allow her followers to affect constructs. The bards who learn these songs take on the title of Voice of Brigh and typically play custom-made instruments that are themselves marvels of engineering and invention.

::ab[Brigh's Knowledge (Ex)]{icon=boost-power passive="A bard adds ‹misc/half› his class level (minimum 1) as a bonus on Knowledge (arcana), Knowledge (dungeoneering), Knowledge (engineering), and Knowledge (religion) checks and can attempt these skill checks untrained. In addition, a Voice of Brigh can affect constructs with his bardic performance, even if they would normally be immune to or unaffected by his performance."}
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="A Voice of Brigh gains the following bardic performances."}

::ab[Brigh's Soothing (Su)]{next icon=power ability="A Voice of Brigh can use Brigh's calming voice to pacify constructs. This functions as the ‹performance/fascinate› bardic performance, except it can target only constructs." replace=Fascinate}

::ab[Brigh's Anger (Su)]{next icon=power l8="A Voice of Brigh can use Brigh's enraged voice to instill constructs with a sense of dread. This functions as the ‹performance/dirge of doom› bardic performance, except it can target only constructs." replace="Dirge of doom"}

::ab[Brigh's Spark (Su)]{next icon=magic l=12 order=an ability="A Voice of Brigh can use Brigh's animating spark to reanimate a destroyed construct. The Voice of Brigh selects a destroyed construct within 60 feet and expends 1 round of bardic performance to reanimate the chosen construct. The construct reanimates with a number of hit points equal to the Voice of Brigh's level, but it is ‹misc/staggered›. The affected construct follows the Voice of Brigh's orders while reanimated." note="Each round, the bard can expend another round of bardic performance to keep that construct reanimated for another round. The construct regains a number of hit points equal to the Voice of Brigh's level for every round it remains reanimated. If the construct recovers all of its hit points in this way, it becomes fully reanimated and no longer requires bardic performance to remain animated. A construct fully reanimated in this way follows the orders of the Voice of Brigh that reanimated it, as if the Voice of Brigh were its original creator, for 24 hours. Afterwards, the construct reverts to its original programming." special="The construct is completely destroyed if the Voice of Brigh ends this performance before the construct regains its full hit points, is interrupted, or is reduced to 0 hit points. A completely destroyed construct cannot be repowered or reanimated, even by another Voice of Brigh. The Voice of Brigh can reanimate multiple constructs with this ability (either at the same time or over successive rounds) but must expend 1 round of bardic performance per reanimated construct per round to maintain the effect." replace="Soothing performance"}

::ab[Brigh's Wrath (Su)]{next icon=lower l14="A Voice of Brigh can use Brigh's furious voice to instill in constructs a fear of destruction. This functions as the ‹performance/frightening tune› bardic performance, except it can target only constructs." replace="Frightening tune"}

voice_of_the_wild.name: Voice of the Wild
description: ## Voice of the Wild
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/81›

Most bards are inspired by the art of civilization, yet the voice of the wild's muse is the grandeur and beauty of nature. The voice of the wild has discovered some of nature's magical secrets, and can use his performance to bring out the bestial side in his allies.

::ab[Wild Knowledge (Ex)]{l=1 passive="A voice of the wild adds ‹misc/half› his level (minimum 1) to all Knowledge (nature) checks. He can use Knowledge (geography) and Knowledge (nature) untrained." icon=boost-power replace="Bardic knowledge"}

::ab[Nature Magic]{icon=learn l1="A voice of the wild can select a 1st-level ‹main/spells_>druid› or ‹main/spells_>ranger› spell as a bard spell known instead of a spell from the bard spell list." l4="The voice of the wild can select another druid or ranger spell (of any bard spell level he can cast)." l7="He can choose another such spell." l10="He can choose another such spell." l13="He can choose another such spell." l16="He can choose another such spell." special="If he chooses to learn a new spell in place of an old one, he can exchange a druid or ranger spell for another." replace="Countersong~versatile performance~jack-of-all-trades"}
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="Voices of the wild gain the following bardic performance."}

::ab[Song of the Wild (Su)]{next icon=boost l3="The voice of the wild can use bardic performance to grant an animal aspect to an ally, as if using the hunter's ‹ability/animal focus› class feature. The ally must be able to hear or see the performance. The voice of the wild uses his bard level as his hunter level for determining the effect of the animal aspect." l10="The bard can affect a second ally with this performance." l17="The bard can affect a third ally." special="Song of the wild relies on audible or visual components." replace="Inspire competence~dirge of doom~inspire heroics"}

wasteland_chronicler.name: Wasteland Chronicler
description: ## Wasteland Chronicler
‹SOURCE People of the Wastes/25›

Some explore the farthest reaches of desolate wastelands, seeking to unlock the mysteries found there and meet the inhabitants of such regions, and chronicle their findings. The wasteland chronicler has discovered a connection to the wastelands and can use his performance to guide himself and others through these regions safely.

::ab[Wasteland Knowledge (Ex)]{icon=boost passive="A wasteland chronicler adds ‹misc/half› his level (minimum 1) as a bonus on Knowledge (geography), Knowledge (local), Knowledge (nature), Knowledge (planes), and Survival checks." replace="Bardic knowledge"}

::ab[Wasteland Specialist (Ex)]{icon=power l3="The wasteland chronicler gains the benefits of the ‹ability/favored terrain› ranger class feature, but the wasteland chronicler can choose only cold, desert, mountain, or swamp from the ranger's favored terrain list. In addition, the wasteland chronicler adds half his level (minimum 1) as a bonus on Diplomacy checks to influence the attitude of wasteland dwellers." l8="He gains another favored terrain." l13="He gains another favored terrain." l18="He gains the final favored terrain." special="If the wasteland chronicler has the favored terrain ability from any other classes, the levels from these different sources stack for the purposes of improving the bonuses of selected favored terrains and choosing new favored terrains." replace="Inspire competence"}

watersinger.name: Watersinger
description: ## Watersinger
‹SOURCE Advanced Race Guide/176›
The watersinger's song reaches from the depths of his soul into the elemental waters from which life first sprang. His voice commands water, bending and shaping it to his desire.
::prereq{r=Undine}

::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="A watersinger has some unique bardic performances, which replace some of the standard bardic performances as listed in each entry. These bardic performances follow all the general rules and restrictions of a bard's bardic performances."}

::ab[Watersong (Su)]{next icon=magic l1="A watersinger can use bardic performance to manipulate and control the shape of water within 30 feet. A successful Perform check allows the bard to animate and control a 5-foot-cube of water. The watersinger can command the water to take various forms, bend, rise, fall, or sustain a shape, and can make it support weight as if it were solid ice. For example, the watersinger could create a pillar of water (to provide cover), ladder, channel, bridge, stairs, slide, and so on. The manipulated water is as slippery as normal ice. This ability cannot create forms more fragile or complex than what could be carved in normal ice. While under the bard's control, the water has hardness 0 and 3 hit points per inch of thickness." l3="The manipulated water gains hardness equal to ‹misc/one-third› the bard's level." l5="The volume affected increases by an additional 5-foot cube for every five bard levels the watersinger has. (These cubes must be adjacent to each other.)" special="The manipulated water retains its shape for 1 round after the bard stops spending bardic performance rounds to maintain it." replace="Fascinate~suggestion~mass suggestion"}

::ab[Waterstrike (Su)]{next icon=melee l3="The watersinger can spend 1 round of bardic performance to command any water he is currently manipulating with his watersong performance to lash out and strike an opponent with a slam attack. The watersinger uses his base attack bonus and Charisma bonus to make this attack, and deals 1d6 points of bludgeoning damage plus his Charisma bonus. The attack can originate from any square of water the bard is manipulating, and the water can get a flanking bonus or help a combatant get one, but cannot make attacks of opportunity. The water can make multiple attacks per round if your base attack bonus allows you to do so." l10="The water's slam damage increases to 1d8 points and the water gains a reach of 10 feet." l15="The water's slam damage increases to 2d6 points." l20="The water's slam damage increases to 2d8 points." replace="Inspire competence"}

::ab[Lifewater (Su)]{next icon=magic l5="The watersinger can spend 1 round of bardic performance as a standard action to manipulate the water, blood, and other fluids within a creature's body, causing the target to become ‹misc/sickened› for 1d4 rounds. Alternatively, he may use this ability to attempt a ‹rule/reposition› combat maneuver, using his base attack bonus and his Charisma modifier as his CMB. This ability has a range of 30 feet, only works on creatures whose bodies contain fluid, and does not affect creatures that are immune to critical hits." replace="The use of *lore master* gained at 5th level" special="A watersinger still gains the use of *lore master* once per day at 11th level and twice per day at 17th."}

::ab[Watersinger Spells]{icon=learn note="A watersinger adds certain water-themed spells to his spell list. He adds these abilities to his spell list as soon as his bard level allows him to cast spells of that spell level." s0="Create water" s1="Hydraulic push~~slipstream" s2="Aqueous orb~~hydraulic torrent~~water walk" s3="Fluid form~~ride the waves" s4="Control water~~communal water walk" s5=Vortex s6=Seamantle}

wit.name: Wit
description: ## Wit
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/23›
The wit is a master of clever repartee and verbal dueling, moving through noble courts like a fish through water.

::ab[Way with Words (Ex)]{icon=boost passive="A wit gains a bonus on Bluff, Diplomacy, Intimidate, Linguistics, and Sense Motive checks. This bonus is equal to 1 + one-fourth his bard level. He also gains a number of edges at the beginning of any ‹rule/verbal duel<s› equal to this bonus." replace="Bardic knowledge"}
::ab[Counterargument]{icon=down ability="A wit can use only Perform (act, comedy, or oratory) to perform a countersong." alter=Countersong}
::ab[Bardic Performance]{head icon=power note="A wit gains the following bardic performance."}

::ab[Cutting Remark (Su)]{next icon=zap l=3 standard="A wit can wound with his words. The wit can spend 1 round of bardic performance to deal 1d4 points of nonlethal damage + the wit's bard level to a creature within 30 feet. Damage reduction applies to this damage." imp8="A target who takes damage from cutting remark is also ‹misc/sickened› for as long as it remains within 30 feet of the wit and for 1 round thereafter. Additionally, the nonlethal damage counts as magic for purposes such as overcoming damage reduction." imp14="A target who takes damage from cutting remark must also succeed at a Will save (DC = 10 + ‹misc/half› the wit's bard level + his Charisma modifier) or be ‹misc/dazed› for 1 round. A creature that makes a successful save is immune to the dazing effect of the wit's cutting remark for 24 hours." special="Cutting remark is a mind-affecting, language-dependent effect, and relies on audible components." replace="Inspire competence~dirge of doom~frightening tune"}

::ab[Quick Witted (Ex)]{icon=power-boost l2="A wit can always act in a surprise round (though he remains ‹rule/flat-footed› until he acts). In addition, the wit adds half his bard level on initiative checks." replace="Versatile performance"}

::ab[On the Ball (Ex)]{icon=roll useInc=bard~6~5 useF="5~1~6" l5="Rather than roll for initiative, the wit can treat his roll as if it was a 10 (adding any modifiers as usual)." l20="When the wit uses this ability, he can instead treat his roll as if it was a 20." replace="Lore master"}

::ab[Duel Master (Ex)]{l=10 icon=boost order=pa passive="The wit does not lose any edges for being at an extreme disadvantage in a verbal duel and can either ask about one additional bias when successfully using Sense Motive to determine an audience's biases or automatically seed one of the biases that he discovered." ability="Once during a verbal duel, he can reassign an appropriate skill from one tactic to another tactic in which he didn't assign any skills. The original tactic is treated as being unprepared for the rest of the duel." replace=Jack-of-all-trades}
